purpose: Companion assumption block for the kot-f1k-dump/1 construction-only moe()-input dump patch, AUTHORED + MOCK-VALIDATED (engineer-1, 2026-07-16) per handoff bead kernel-of-truth-xq0h item 2 against the FROZEN F1-K experiment (frozen_sha256 505165ee, commit b332edb6). EMITTED for central registration by the coordinator in registry/assumptions.jsonl with the landing commit; this pass writes NO registry entry. The patch is a DRAFT: it applies on top of the gate-0 KaE patch (11f8b458) at colibri base a78a06fc, is inert unless KAE_DUMP is set, MUST pass a gate-0 Codex review before any real Modal run, and nothing here touched a governed model, weights, Modal, or any frozen record. Ids 2485..2491 are the next-free integers after ASM-2484 (repo-wide grep incl. the concurrent asm-s4speed block ending at 2484, 2026-07-16). REVISION-2 (2026-07-16): manifest parser hardened per gate-0 review item 5 (garbage lines + trailing junk now abort; 2 new measured negatives) and locked-range references corrected per item 7 — the seven assumption rows below are unchanged from r1.
backing_artifacts: poc/glm52-probe/f1k-harness/dump-patch/kot-f1k-dump.patch (unified diff vs the KaE-patched tree; REVISION-2 sha256 fb5d2f3558351f608afccb29ab974aac2a62182ad67f27ec5c3c5f3e9eab0097; regenerated via git diff --cached on the pinned KaE tree so the index post-image hashes match the applied blobs — payload hunks unchanged from the reviewed r2)

poc/glm52-probe/f1k-harness/dump-patch/real-checks.log (REAL-SOURCE CHECKS OK: applies on pinned KaE tree, builds, test_kae 44/44 regression, test_kae_dump 43/43, objdump per-function inertness: only moe+main differ of 79 shared)

assumptions.claim: The frozen contract phrase 'the SUM ... in the engine's own f32' is read as: the SUMMANDS are the engine's own f32 hidden-state components (untouched, no re-quantization); accumulation is IEEE-754 float64 over gated positions in ascending absolute-position order; sums are cast to f32 only at KAED write. This matches the repo mock mock_colibri_dump.py cell-for-cell (Python float == C double; struct '<f' == (float) cast), so the $0 pipeline and the real engine share one arithmetic contract. Flagged as gate-0 open question 1 for explicit confirmation.
assumptions.rationale: The mock is the pinned $0 emission-surface oracle for the construction pipeline; diverging from its arithmetic (e.g. pure-f32 accumulation) would make mock and real engines disagree at the bit level and lose precision at D=6144 scale.
assumptions.backing_ref: kae_dump.h header comment + kaed_capture/kaed_write_line; validate.py V2 (192 cells bit-exact) [MEASURED at mock scale]

assumptions.claim: The dump state g_kdump is a file-scope global in glm.c, deliberately NOT a Model field, so sizeof(Model), model_init and layer_forward stay byte-identical to the gate-0 KaE engine. Consequently the machine-level inertness gate for THIS patch is: of all shared functions between the KaE-only and KaE+dump objects, only {moe, main} may differ (clone symbols like moe.constprop.0 count as their base function; newly-outlined symbols such as kae_load/run_score/kaed_arm are acceptable inlining artifacts). MEASURED on gcc 11.5 -O2 -march=x86-64-v3: 79 shared functions, exactly {moe, main} differ, none removed.
assumptions.rationale: Bounds what the additive patch can touch at machine level; a smaller allowed-diff set than the KaE patch's {moe, layer_forward, main, model_init} because moe()'s signature is unchanged and no struct grows.
assumptions.backing_ref: real-checks.sh step 6; real-checks.log; PATCH-NOTES.md open question 3; kot-f1k-dump.patch sha256=fb5d2f3558351f608afccb29ab974aac2a62182ad67f27ec5c3c5f3e9eab0097

assumptions.claim: Engine-side PHASE SEPARATION: KAE_DUMP is mutually exclusive with KAE_SCORE and with KAE=1, enforced fail-closed in main() BEFORE model load (nonzero exit, never degrade). A dump with the splice armed would record the spliced (not plain) model and silently corrupt every carrier; a dump mixed with scoring would violate the frozen contract's mutual exclusion. The dumped states are therefore always the UNMODIFIED model's, as §2.4 requires (mean difference between plain WITH/WITHOUT prefills).
assumptions.rationale: Harness-side exclusion (run_dump pops KAE_SCORE) is not sufficient defense in depth for a paid construction run; the engine refuses the contaminated configuration itself.
assumptions.backing_ref: glm.c phase-separation guard (patched lines 2793-2801); validate.py V5 cases 1-2 [MEASURED nonzero exits]; kot-f1k-dump.patch sha256=fb5d2f3558351f608afccb29ab974aac2a62182ad67f27ec5c3c5f3e9eab0097

assumptions.claim: PER-SLOT GATED-COUNT INVARIANT: after each manifest line's prefill, every dump slot must have accumulated EXACTLY gated_count rows (the manifest's span>=0 count), else the engine aborts nonzero before writing the line. This fail-closes three silent-corruption modes: a requested dump layer that never reaches moe() (dense layer / never-executed layer id), a prefill that visits a position more than once, and (via the sticky capture-error flag) a hidden-dim mismatch. For the real A(iv) union 3..78 this doubles as the check that every requested layer is a genuine MoE layer on the real model.
assumptions.rationale: Without this, a dump layer outside the sparse set would emit all-zero sums that still parse downstream; the round-8/round-10 hardening history shows all-zero bodies previously satisfied naive verifies.
assumptions.backing_ref: kae_dump.h kaed_write_line; test_kae_dump.c Test D; validate.py V5 case 9 [MEASURED]; kot-f1k-dump.patch sha256=fb5d2f3558351f608afccb29ab974aac2a62182ad67f27ec5c3c5f3e9eab0097

assumptions.claim: run_kae_dump treats EVERY malformed manifest line (bad T, bad token id, bad span slot, zero gated positions, a wholly non-numeric/garbage line, trailing junk after the required 2T+1 integers, line-count mismatch) as a fatal nonzero-exit error, deliberately DIVERGING from run_kae_score's per-item skip-and-continue: construction integrity over availability. A silently dropped or partially-parsed construction pass would shift the 16-context §2.4 means without any downstream detector, whereas a scoring item skip is visible in the per-item output stream.
assumptions.rationale: build_carriers fails closed on nonzero engine exit, so engine-side abort converts silent corruption into loud failure at $0 cost.
assumptions.backing_ref: validate.log (recorded MOCK VALIDATION 34/34, nonzero-exit negatives); glm.c run_kae_dump; validate.py V5 cases 7-8 (zero-gated line, malformed token id) and 10-11 (garbage line, trailing junk — the r2 negatives) [MEASURED nonzero exits]; PATCH-NOTES.md hunk 4; kot-f1k-dump.patch sha256=fb5d2f3558351f608afccb29ab974aac2a62182ad67f27ec5c3c5f3e9eab0097

assumptions.claim: The kot-f1k-tok/1 wrapper (tok_glm52.py) validates every emitted offset pair as a Python-str CHARACTER range (0 <= start <= end <= len(text), one pair per id) and ABORTS the whole batch on any violation, because a byte-indexed or normalizer-shifted offset would gate the wrong tokens and corrupt every carrier silently. Wrapper mechanics are selftest-PASS on a constructed multibyte tokenizer; the REAL GLM-5.2 tokenizer.json (a) sha-pin (TOK_SHA256, ASM-1971) and (b) wrapper-vs-engine-prefill id consistency remain BRING-UP obligations on Modal before any real dump.
assumptions.rationale: The frozen tokenizer contract demands Python-str char offsets and the template-spec intersection rule; the wrapper cannot be validated against the real tokenizer at $0 because tokenizer.json ships with the 384GB weights snapshot.
assumptions.backing_ref: tok_glm52.py; tok-selftest.log [MEASURED, mock scope]; build_carriers.py TOKENIZER CONTRACT

assumptions.claim: The KAED writer emits host-endian i32/f32 and is correct only on little-endian x86-64 — the SAME documented platform assumption as the gate-0 kae.h KAEC format and the pinned Modal/x86 build boxes. No byte-swapping is implemented; a big-endian host would fail the harness's magic/geometry checks loudly, not silently.
assumptions.rationale: Consistency with the registered KAEC assumption; all registered compute paths (Modal CPU boxes, this box) are x86-64.
assumptions.backing_ref: kae_dump.h header comment; kae.h 'File formats (host-endian; little-endian x86-64 assumed)'
